title: adhatoda protracta
intro: Adhatoda protracta, also known as Vasaka, is a plant species belonging to the Acanthaceae family and is native to the Indian subcontinent. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and cough. The health benefits of Adhatoda protracta include its ability to relieve respiratory congestion, soothe sore throats, and reduce fever. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
